summary: Determinate progress atom with optional label and percentage text, small/default Figma track sizes, and shared progressbar semantics across adapters.
philosophy: Keep ProgressBar as read-only status display, separate from Slider and SliderField input semantics.
Keep value normalization and accessibility metadata in the core recipe so adapters only render the shared contract.
Keep visual treatment in preset CSS through stable mw-progress-bar classes and theme-relative variables.

useWhen: The UI needs to communicate determinate completion, upload, import, or setup progress.
A visible label and percentage should be displayed together with a compact track.
React, Vue, and Svelte consumers need the same read-only progress semantics.
avoidWhen: The user should adjust the value; use Slider or SliderField instead.
The state is indeterminate; use Spinner or a loading-specific composition instead.
The surrounding UI cannot provide a truthful accessible name when the visible label is hidden.

automationCovers: Shared React/Vue adapter contract tests for progressbar value metadata, clamping, label and percentage visibility, and disabled state metadata.
Svelte adapter tests for progressbar rendering and small hidden-label usage.
Storybook introduction/taxonomy tests and a11y smoke coverage in React, Vue, and Svelte Storybook apps.
manualReviewRequired: Visual review against the synced light and dark Figma progress-bar frames.
Product-level review when hiding the visible label so the accessible name remains truthful.
notes: The dashboard teaser uses ProgressBar to avoid presenting a read-only completion value as an adjustable SliderField.

openQuestions: Should future design sources expose semantic progress intent colors beyond brand progress?
